title: Peptic Ulcer Disease
chief_complaint: I have burning pain in my stomach that wakes me up at 2am. It gets better when I eat but comes back 2-3 hours later. I've been taking ibuprofen daily for my back pain.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Epigastric tenderness, no guarding, no rebound. Hemoccult positive stool.

hidden_data.egds: Duodenal ulcer 1.5cm in duodenal bulb with visible vessel (Forrest IIa). No active bleeding. H. pylori CLO test: POSITIVE.

correct_diagnosis: Duodenal Ulcer with H. pylori Infection and Iron Deficiency Anemia from Chronic Blood Loss
key_learning_points: Duodenal ulcer = pain 2-3h after meals + nocturnal pain + relieved by food. Gastric ulcer = pain worse with food
H. pylori causes 70% of duodenal ulcers. NSAIDs cause 30%
Forrest classification: Ia (spurting), Ib (oozing), IIa (visible vessel = 43% rebleed), IIb (adherent clot), IIc (flat spot), III (clean base)
Treatment: Triple therapy (PPI + Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin ×14 days) or Quadruple (PPI + Bismuth + Tetracycline + Metronidazole)
Stop NSAIDs. Repeat endoscopy in 8 weeks to confirm healing (rule out malignancy for gastric ulcers)

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Duodenal ulcer with H. pylori confirmed. Triple therapy + PPI and stopping NSAIDs is the correct management.
feedback.attending_note: Start Pantoprazole 40mg BID + Amoxicillin 1g BID + Clarithromycin 500mg BID ×14 days. Stop ibuprofen. Iron supplementation. Repeat endoscopy if symptoms persist. Test of cure (urea breath test) 4 weeks after completing therapy.
